name: Cabinet
description: A lightweight self-hosted file cabinet and document manager.
summary: Cabinet (v0.2.12, latest release Apr 10, 2026) is an AI-first, self-hosted knowledge base and 'startup OS' built on Next.js 16 and TypeScript, where all data is stored as plain markdown files on disk with no database. It features AI agent orchestration with scheduled cron jobs, git-backed version history, an embedded HTML app renderer, and a web terminal — making it a more powerful alternative to Obsidian or Notion for developer teams. The project is open source under the MIT license with 13 releases and an active community on Discord. See the full release history at https://github.com/hilash/cabinet.

answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — 13 releases, latest v0.2.12 on Apr 10, 2026, 172 commits, active Discord community
answers.Does it support user authentication?: Basic password protection via KB_PASSWORD environment variable; leave empty for no auth
answers.Is there a Docker image available?: A .dockerignore file is present suggesting Docker support, but no official Docker image is explicitly documented in the changelog

breaking_excerpts: See CHANGELOG.md for breaking changes (referenced but not included in the provided text)
Project is described as 'moving fast' with rapidly evolving roadmap, implying frequent breaking changes between releases

alternatives.name: Paperless-ngx
alternatives.review: Paperless-ngx offers far more powerful OCR, tagging, and document indexing than Cabinet but is significantly heavier and more complex to self-host.

alternatives.name: Teedy
alternatives.review: Teedy provides a polished document management UI with workflows and permissions but requires more resources and setup compared to Cabinet's simplicity.

alternatives.name: Docspell
alternatives.review: Docspell excels at automated document processing and classification but has a steeper learning curve and heavier stack than Cabinet.

alternatives.name: Filestash
alternatives.review: Filestash is similarly lightweight and focuses on file browsing across many storage backends but lacks dedicated document management and metadata features.

alternatives.name: Nextcloud
alternatives.review: Nextcloud is a comprehensive file and collaboration platform with document management plugins but is far heavier and more complex than Cabinet's focused scope.
